docs.title: Verification history
docs.text:
• 2026-04-20: page created. Decompile pass against E:\Steam\steamapps\common\Stationeers\rocketstation_Data\Managed\Assembly-CSharp.dll via ILSpy. All section stamps set to 0.2.6228.27061.
  • 2026-04-20: re-verified RunningTargetPressure definition. Added verbatim private static readonly PressurekPa RunningTargetPressure = new PressurekPa(4000.0) quote, HandleShutDown Lerp quote, and PressurekPa unit convention note to the "Gas output and venting" section. No sibling target-pressure constants exist. Section stamp unchanged (same version, same day).
  • 2026-04-20: re-verified the step-of-10 rounding claim on both lever setters. Setter bodies unchanged from the page's existing quotes; order is Clamp([0, 100]) then Round(value / 10f) * 10f. CombustionDeepMiner.SetLogicValue is a pass-through cast from double to float with no intermediate rounding. No additional Float16 quantization on the network-serialization path for Throttle / CombustionLimiter (both only set NetworkUpdateFlags bits, 4096 and 8192 respectively). Unity's Mathf.Round uses banker's rounding per Unity docs, but the governor never writes half-values so the behavior at .5 is not exercised.
  • 2026-04-24: added "Atmosphere / GasMixture access for chamber composition" subsection. Verbatim verified: Atmosphere.CombustionEnergy is a public field (public MoleEnergy CombustionEnergy = MoleEnergy.Zero;) not a property; per-gas ratios are read via the method GasMixture.GetGasTypeRatio(Chemistry.GasType) returning float, with no per-gas Ratio properties on either Atmosphere or GasMixture; InternalCombustion._normalCombustionEnergyCache is a private MoleEnergy field on the global-namespace type, resolvable via Type.GetType("InternalCombustion, Assembly-CSharp") and snapshots atm.CombustionEnergy once per tick before TryCombust runs. Resolves bugs in DeepMinerLogger v0.1.0 where ratio reads and CombustionEnergy reads were returning 0 because they searched for properties that did not exist. Additive content; no contradiction with existing claims.
  • 2026-04-24: full-class decompile pass of CombustionDeepMiner and InternalCombustion. Added a new "Additional miner-level findings (full-class decompile 2026-04-24)" section capturing findings not previously documented: CanMine() verbatim (two conditions: ThingInTheWay == null AND _deepMinables != null, nothing else), CombustionDeepMiner.AssessError override (mirrors IsOperable auto-toggle on a second path, gated on GameManager.RunSimulation), CombustionDeepMinerSaveData four-field persistence (Throttle, CombustionLimiter, Rpm, Stress), network update flag 16384 for Rpm (completing the flag set), InteractWith Button4-7 mappings (Throttle/CombustionLimiter ± 10), motor audio silencing at RPM < 2, Awake as the only init override, confirmation that no damage/wear state exists on the miner, and confirmation that InputNetwork2 is structurally present but never consumed by HandleGasInput or checked by IsOperable/AssessError. Flagged an ordering issue in the page: "Error = 1 trigger conditions", "HandleShutDown implementation", "Stress dial display (real-time behavior)", and "Stress audio thresholds (refined)" were appended after "Verification history" by an earlier session and should be moved above VH in a future cleanup pass, consolidating overlapping material with the "Error states and shutdown" section. No contradictions with existing verified content. New open questions: where _deepMinables is assigned (likely DeepMiner.OnRegistered / IGenerateMinables), where ThingInTheWay is set (likely DeepMiner.CheckThingInWay() called from DrillDownAnimation), where InteractError bit is defined on base Device.
  • 2026-04-24: expanded "Error states and shutdown" with a dedicated "IsInputValid / IsOutputValid: structural, no pressure minimum" subsection. Verbatim decompiled IsInputValid, IsOutputValid, IsInput2Valid from DeviceInputOutput; confirmed no override anywhere in the chain (DeviceInputOutputImport, DeviceInputOutputImportExport, DeviceInputOutputImportExportCircuit, DeepMiner, CombustionDeepMiner). Documented the three structural checks (network not null, IsNetworkValid = StructureList count > 0, not IsAwaitingEvent), and explicitly noted what the property does NOT check: no minimum pressure, no mole count, no gas-type, no matter-state, no temperature. Flagged the IsInput2Valid quirk: secondary input has its own validity property, but CombustionDeepMiner.IsOperable gates only on primary IsInputValid/IsOutputValid, not IsInput2Valid; HandleGasInput also consumes only from the primary. Additive content under already-stamped "Error states and shutdown" section; no contradiction. Resolves the open question "does low input pipe pressure trigger Error=1" - it does not.
  • 2026-04-24: added "Error states and shutdown" section with verbatim CombustionDeepMiner.IsOperable override, verbatim InternalCombustion.HandleShutDown, and the complete list of six Error = 1 triggers (five via IsOperable auto-toggle, one via SpeedTick stress branch). Previous page coverage was limited to the stress-trip case and a single "Shutdown RPM decay" row in the constants table; the IsOperable auto-toggle and the non-stress Error paths were undocumented, and the claim that stress=100 "knocks IsOperable false" in the "Per-tick rules" bullet list was incorrect (stress sets Error directly in SpeedTick; IsOperable is a separate gate over physical conditions and auto-clears Error next tick unless a physical condition also holds). Corrected the bullet, added cross-reference, added "Stress dial and audio" subsection documenting the 2/sec lerp on the needle (explains why brief stress spikes are invisible on the dial while sustained stress=100 is visible within ~1.5 s). Stress audio thresholds updated from >= to > conditions with the 90-95 hysteresis band. Additive section; no contradiction with existing verified content beyond the stress/IsOperable clarification. Added to sources: CombustionDeepMiner.IsOperable, DeviceInputOutput.IsInputValid, DeviceInputOutput.IsOutputValid, DeepMiner.CanMine, InternalCombustion.HandleShutDown. Section stamps: "SpeedTick: RPM, friction, stress" and "Error states and shutdown" at 0.2.6228.27061 @ 2026-04-24.
  • 2026-04-24: conflict on "SpeedTick energy gate units". Previous claim (across "SpeedTick: RPM, friction, stress", "Startup transient", and "Constants summary"): MoleEnergy(30.0) = 30 MJ (30,000,000 J). New finding: MoleEnergy(30.0) = 30 J. Fresh validator verdict: B is correct, gate is 30 J. Verbatim evidence: public MoleEnergy(double value) { _value = value; } stores verbatim with no multiplier; combustionEnergy = new MoleEnergy(mole.Enthalpy() * mole2.EnthalpyMultiplier * mole.Quantity.ToDouble()) in CombustionResult.RunCombustion where Mole.Enthalpy(Hydrogen) => 306000.0 (J/mol), so CombustionEnergy is in SI joules. Result: corrected "30 MJ" / "30 MoleEnergy" to "30 J" in all six locations, added explanatory parentheticals on the MoleEnergy unit convention, added CombustionResult and MoleEnergy to sources. Practical consequence: the gate is trivially clearable (0.0001 mol of H2 combustion clears it), so it effectively only closes when the chamber is empty of fuel. This weakens the "Throttle=0 shuts combustion in N seconds" argument in "Startup transient" only quantitatively (the gate does eventually close but via chamber draining, not via the gate being tight). Sections restamped: SpeedTick, Startup transient, Constants summary.
  • 2026-04-24: resolved CombustionInput semantics in "Logic variables". Decompiled Assets.Scripts.Objects.Pipes.DeviceInputOutput.GetLogicValue (and sibling DeviceInput.GetLogicValue) against E:\Steam\steamapps\common\Stationeers\rocketstation_Data\Managed\Assembly-CSharp.dll via ilspycmd. Both implementations read CombustionInput as InputNetwork.Atmosphere.Inflamed ? 1 : 0, a boolean flame-present flag. This is not a fuel potency / enthalpy / combustion-energy reading; it is true only while the input pipe's gas mixture is actively burning (set inside Atmosphere.TryCombust, cleared by Atmosphere.ResetCombustionData). Corrected the table entry previously grouped with the quantitative input reads. Section stamp on "Logic variables" bumped to 2026-04-24.
  • 2026-04-22: clarified the num Lerp clamping in "SpeedTick: RPM, friction, stress". Mathf.Clamp01 is on the lerp fraction T/5000, not on T itself; chamber temperature can rise arbitrarily above 5000 K while num saturates at 12. No code change; the verbatim SpeedTick quote already contains this, the sentence summarising it was ambiguous ("maxing at 12 RPM/tick near 5000 K" read as if T were also capped). Reinforces the "Temperature above 5000 K is wasted fuel" intuition used by the control algorithm. Section stamp unchanged (same day).
  • 2026-04-22: corrected steady-state RPM formula and mismatch formula in "SpeedTick: RPM, friction, stress". Previous text said Rpm_steady = 100 * num and mismatch = |num - 0.01*Rpm|. Re-derivation from the verbatim SpeedTick code (num2 = Rpm + num, num4 = num2 * 0.99, Rpm = num4, num5 = num2 - num4, num6 = |num - num5|) gives Rpm_ss = 99 * num and mismatch = |0.99*num - 0.01*Rpm|. Max RPM at num=12 is 1188, not 1200. No decompile; sign-error in the algebra on the existing code quote. Section stamp on "SpeedTick: RPM, friction, stress" bumped to 2026-04-22. Downstream sections ("Startup transient" tables and values) use the slightly-off formulas; differences are ~1% in stress-per-tick values and unchanged qualitative conclusions, so those sections are not restamped pending a fuller pass.
  • 2026-04-21: added "Startup transient: why a hot chamber explodes on any throttle" section. Derived analysis from existing SpeedTick quotes in "SpeedTick: RPM, friction, stress"; no new decompile. Documents the temperature-only dependence of num, the stress/sec table as a function of chamber T, why CombustionLimiter and Throttle = 10 cannot stop a hot-chamber runaway, why Throttle = 0 takes multiple seconds to close the combustion gates, the mechanics that make the user's manual pulse strategy work, the safe cold-start regime, and governor implications for IC10 scripts that must start on a hot chamber.
  • 2026-04-21: re-decompiled CombustionDeepMiner, DeepMiner, InternalCombustion, DeviceInputOutput, DeviceAtmospherics, Pipes.Device, IInternalCombustion against the same DLL. Extended the "Class hierarchy" section with the full chain (CombustionDeepMiner -> DeepMiner -> DeviceInputOutputImportExportCircuit -> DeviceInputOutputImportExport -> DeviceInputOutputImport -> DeviceInputOutput -> DeviceAtmospherics -> Device) and the per-class ownership of state. Corrected the InitInternalAtmosphere source: it is a CombustionDeepMiner override with its own private static readonly VolumeLitres Volume = new VolumeLitres(2.0) field, not inherited from DeepMiner. Added "Drill depth and ore yield" section with verbatim DeepMiner.DrillDownAnimation, ProgressProcessing, IsSpawnTime, ResetSpawnTime, SpawnOre, RpmNormalised quotes; resolves the prior open question. Extended "Gas output and venting" with the DeviceAtmospherics.pressurePerTick = 101.325f default and the pipe-side vent-rate cap. Extended "Tick pipeline" with HandleGasInput gating on Error <= 0 and the RPM half-life calculation for lag after a limiter/throttle change. Extended "Logic variables" with explicit rows for Temperature, Pressure, TotalMoles, RatioXxx, On, and a verbatim excerpt of Device.CanLogicRead / Device.GetLogicValue showing that HasReadableAtmosphere => true on CombustionDeepMiner routes those reads to the chamber's internal atmosphere. Added STANDARD_RPM, DRILL_DOWN_SPEED, RpmNormalised, shutdown RPM decay, pipe-side vent rate, stress-audio thresholds, and dial-wobble constants to the "Constants summary" table.

docs.title: Open questions
docs.text: • Liquid fuels in a combustion chamber: Mole.Enthalpy returns the same energy for Hydrogen and LiquidHydrogen, but the Combust method subtracts a latent-heat term (IdealGas.Energy(burned_fuel_moles, fuel.LatentHeat)) when the fuel matter-state is liquid. Net effect on deep-miner RPM accel was not measured in this pass.

docs.title: Error = 1 trigger conditions
docs.text:
Every path that sets Error = 1 on CombustionDeepMiner:

1. Stress reaches 100 (SpeedTick) — requires stress to hit 100: 

// InternalCombustion.SpeedTick
if (Stress >= 100f)
{
    Rpm -= 40f;
    if (Parent.GetAsThing.Error == 0)
    {
        OnServer.Interact(Parent.GetAsThing.InteractError, 1);
    }
}

2. IsOperable property returns false — does NOT require stress to hit 100. Triggered when any of these conditions are true: 

// CombustionDeepMiner.IsOperable (property getter)
bool flag = (bool)base.ProgrammableChip && (CodeErrorState != 0 || base.ProgrammableChip.CompilationError);
bool flag2 = base.IsInputValid && base.IsOutputValid && !flag && CanMine() && base.IsStructureCompleted;
if (Error == 0 && !flag2)
{
    OnServer.Interact(base.InteractError, 1);
}

This sets Error = 1 when ANY of these are true: - !base.IsInputValid: input pipe disconnected or broken - !base.IsOutputValid: output pipe disconnected or broken - CodeErrorState != 0: ProgrammableChip has a runtime error - base.ProgrammableChip.CompilationError: ProgrammableChip failed to compile - !CanMine(): either ThingInTheWay != null OR _deepMinables == null - !base.IsStructureCompleted: structure blueprint not complete

3. OnAtmosphericTick immediately calls HandleShutDown when Error=1 — this is a consequence, not a trigger: 

// CombustionDeepMiner.OnAtmosphericTick (line 226 in decompile)
if (!OnOff || !Powered || Error == 1)
{
    _internalCombustion.HandleShutDown();
}

4. AssessError (called from DeepMiner base class) — duplicate of IsOperable logic: 

// CombustionDeepMiner.AssessError override
public override void AssessError()
{
    if (GameManager.RunSimulation)
    {
        bool flag = (bool)base.ProgrammableChip && (CodeErrorState != 0 || base.ProgrammableChip.CompilationError);
        bool flag2 = base.IsInputValid && base.IsOutputValid && !flag && CanMine() && base.IsStructureCompleted;
        if (Error == 0 && !flag2)
        {
            OnServer.Interact(base.InteractError, 1);
        }
        else if (Error == 1 && flag2)
        {
            OnServer.Interact(base.InteractError, 0);
        }
    }
}

docs.title: HandleShutDown implementation
docs.text:
// InternalCombustion.HandleShutDown
public void HandleShutDown()
{
    if (!_gainedStress)
    {
        Rpm -= 15f;
        _targetPressure = RocketMath.Lerp(PressurekPa.Zero, RunningTargetPressure, Mathf.Clamp01(Rpm / 1000f));
    }
}

Behavior when shutdown is active: - If _gainedStress is false: decays RPM by 15 per tick, and _targetPressure slides from 4000 kPa down to 0 as RPM falls toward 0. - Stress continues to decay at 0.5/tick in SpeedTick (handled before the shutdown branch). - Stress does NOT freeze; it decays automatically when the mismatch condition clears. - Chamber continues to vent via HandleGasOutput (line 228 in OnAtmosphericTick, which runs AFTER the HandleShutDown call). - Chamber does NOT admit new gas: HandleGasInput (line 229) is gated on Error <= 0, so when Error = 1, no fuel enters.

Consequence: when a miner crashes due to any non-stress Error = 1, the chamber fuel load drains at whatever burn-fraction rate CombustionLimiter specifies (minimum 0.1%, maximum 75%), while RPM decays by 15/tick (4.3x faster than the running friction-only decay of 1%/tick) and venting runs freely.

docs.title: Stress dial display (real-time behavior)
docs.text:
The stress dial is smoothed with Lerp, NOT driven by live instantaneous stress value:

// Dial.UpdatePosition (called from CombustionDeepMiner.UpdateEachFrame every frame)
public void UpdatePosition(float target, float deltaTime)
{
    float t = Mathf.Clamp01(target / maxValue);
    float b = Mathf.Lerp(needleMinimum, needleMaximum, t);
    _lastAngle = Mathf.Lerp(_lastAngle, b, deltaTime * lerpSpeed);
    _needleTransform.localRotation = Quaternion.AngleAxis(_lastAngle, axisOfRotation);
}
  • lerpSpeed = 2f (hardcoded constant in Dial.cs).
  • Each frame: _lastAngle moves toward the target angle at rate Mathf.Lerp(..., deltaTime * 2.0f).
  • At 60 FPS (deltaTime ≈ 0.0167 s): dial moves 0.0167 * 2 = 0.0334 = 3.34% of remaining distance per frame.
  • Time to reach 63% of target: ~1 frame at 60 FPS.
  • Time to reach 95% of target: ~89 frames ≈ 1.48 seconds.

Consequence: A stress spike from 0 to 100 that occurs in a single 0.05 s atmospheric tick (while the dial updates at 60 FPS in the render loop) would: - Update the dial once per frame during the spike. - On the frame when stress = 100: dial angle = Lerp(previous, 100_degrees, 0.0167 * 2) ≈ 3.3% of the way to full scale. - Next frame, stress is already decaying (0.5/tick in SpeedTick). - Dial would show a barely-visible upward twitch, then slowly creep downward.

The player's observation that "stress dial stays low the whole time... never visibly climbs" is consistent with stress spiking above 100 and immediately crashing the miner, leaving the dial showing only the low mid-decay remainder before shutdown fully kicks in.

docs.title: Stress audio thresholds (refined)
docs.text:
// CombustionDeepMiner.HandleStressAnimSound
if (_internalCombustion.Stress > 40f)
{
    float t = Mathf.Clamp01((_internalCombustion.Stress - 50f) / 50f);
    num = Mathf.Lerp(0.3f, 1f, t);
    // ... trigger / update stress audio
}
else if (_stressAudio.IsPlaying)
{
    _stressAudio.Stop();
}

if (_internalCombustion.Stress < 90f && _maxStressAudio.IsPlaying)
{
    _maxStressAudio.Stop();
}
else if (_internalCombustion.Stress > 95f && !_maxStressAudio.IsPlaying)
{
    _maxStressAudio.Trigger();
}
  • Warning audio (steady hum): triggers when Stress > 40f, volume ramps from 0.3 to 1.0 as stress goes from 50 to 100.
  • Max-stress audio (loud alarm): triggers when Stress > 95f, stops when Stress < 90f (hysteresis band).

The page's summary of "warn at 40, loud band 50..95, max-stress at 95" is slightly loose; the precise conditions are Stress > 40 for warn-trigger and Stress > 95 for max-stress.

docs.title: Additional miner-level findings (full-class decompile 2026-04-24)
docs.text: This section captures findings from a whole-class decompile pass of CombustionDeepMiner and InternalCombustion that are not covered by the sections above. Note: the ordering of this page is currently inconsistent because earlier sessions appended content after ## Verification history; a future pass should move "Error = 1 trigger conditions", "HandleShutDown implementation", "Stress dial display (real-time behavior)", "Stress audio thresholds (refined)", and this section up into their logical positions before Verification History, and consolidate overlapping material with the earlier "Error states and shutdown" section.

docs.title: CanMine verbatim
docs.text:
// Assembly-CSharp.dll :: Assets.Scripts.Objects.Pipes.DeepMiner.CanMine
public bool CanMine()
{
    if (!ThingInTheWay)
    {
        return _deepMinables != null;
    }
    return false;
}

Two conditions only: no physical obstruction (ThingInTheWay == null) AND an ore-generation table is set (_deepMinables != null). No export-chute check, no bedrock check, no silo-fill check, no power check (power is gated separately via Powered in OnAtmosphericTick's second shutdown branch). A mining miner that stops because !CanMine() went false is either obstructed or lost its minable table; those are the only two levers.

_deepMinables is assigned once per world region from WorldSetting.Current.Data.DeepMinablesData on registration; the exact assignment site was not re-verified in this pass. ThingInTheWay is checked in DeepMiner.CheckThingInWay() (also not re-verified) and referenced from DrillDownAnimation. Both remain as open questions for a targeted decompile.

docs.title: AssessError is a second path that mirrors IsOperable
docs.text:
// Assembly-CSharp.dll :: Assets.Scripts.Objects.Pipes.CombustionDeepMiner.AssessError
public override void AssessError()
{
    if (GameManager.RunSimulation)
    {
        bool flag = (bool)base.ProgrammableChip && (CodeErrorState != 0 || base.ProgrammableChip.CompilationError);
        bool flag2 = base.IsInputValid && base.IsOutputValid && !flag && CanMine() && base.IsStructureCompleted;
        if (Error == 0 && !flag2)
            OnServer.Interact(base.InteractError, 1);
        else if (Error == 1 && flag2)
            OnServer.Interact(base.InteractError, 0);
    }
}

AssessError is a base-class-driven hook that duplicates the IsOperable auto-toggle logic verbatim, gated on GameManager.RunSimulation. This means Error auto-set/clear fires from two paths per tick (once from IsOperable in OnAtmosphericTick, once from AssessError in whatever base tick calls it). Net behaviour is idempotent because both sides apply the same branch conditions; the redundancy matters only as noise in the Interact history.

docs.title: CombustionDeepMinerSaveData persists four fields
docs.text:
public class CombustionDeepMinerSaveData : DeepMinerSaveData
{
    public float Throttle;
    public float CombustionLimiter;
    public float Rpm;
    public float Stress;
}

InitialiseSaveData writes these four; DeserializeSave reads them back in the same order. SerializeOnJoin / DeserializeOnJoin transmit the same four on network join, same order. No chamber-atmosphere persistence sits on CombustionDeepMiner specifically; whatever DeviceAtmospherics saves for InternalAtmosphere applies through the base path.

Saved -> loaded invariants: Throttle, CombustionLimiter, Rpm, Stress all survive save/load and rejoin. A reloaded miner resumes at its persisted RPM and stress; it does not cold-start.

docs.title: Network update flags: RPM uses 16384
docs.text:
Completing the flag set previously documented (4096 Throttle, 8192 CombustionLimiter, 32768 Stress):

Bit Field Setter trigger 4096 Throttle Throttle setter 8192 CombustionLimiter CombustionLimiter setter 16384 Rpm Rpm setter on the server 32768 Stress Stress setter on the server

No other flags. No custom RPCs. ProcessUpdate applies the four fields directly to _internalCombustion on the client side; no interpolation, no prediction. The dial's visual lerp (rate 2/sec) is the only smoothing anywhere in the stack.

docs.title: Player interaction buttons route through the same setters as logic
docs.text:
// CombustionDeepMiner.InteractWith (button4-7 only)
case Button4: _internalCombustion.Throttle            = Mathf.Clamp(_internalCombustion.Throttle            - 10f, 0f, 100f); break;
case Button5: _internalCombustion.Throttle            = Mathf.Clamp(_internalCombustion.Throttle            + 10f, 0f, 100f); break;
case Button6: _internalCombustion.CombustionLimiter   = Mathf.Clamp(_internalCombustion.CombustionLimiter   - 10f, 0f, 100f); break;
case Button7: _internalCombustion.CombustionLimiter   = Mathf.Clamp(_internalCombustion.CombustionLimiter   + 10f, 0f, 100f); break;

Matters: manual button interactions and IC10 sb ... Throttle writes hit the same property setter. Lever animation, step-of-10 rounding, and network-flag emission fire identically regardless of path.

docs.title: Motor sound gated on RPM >= 2
docs.text:
// CombustionDeepMiner sound update
if (IsOccluded || Rpm < 2f || !base.IsStructureCompleted || !OnOff || !Powered)
{
    _motorAudio.UpdatePlayState(shouldPlay: false);
}

The motor audio silences at RPM < 2. Low relevance for gameplay but useful when debugging - "the miner is silent" does not imply "the miner is off"; it can also mean RPM decayed to < 2 via friction.

docs.title: Awake is the only init override; _internalCombustion is constructed here
docs.text:
public override void Awake()
{
    base.Awake();
    if (!IsCursor)
    {
        _internalCombustion = new InternalCombustion(this);
    }
    dial?.Init();
    _gear1Audio = GetAudioEvent(Animator.StringToHash("GearTwo"));
    _gear3Audio = GetAudioEvent(Animator.StringToHash("GearFour"));
    _stressAudio = GetAudioEvent(Animator.StringToHash("Stress"));
    _maxStressAudio = GetAudioEvent(Animator.StringToHash("MaxStress"));
    _motorAudio = GetAudioEvent(Animator.StringToHash("MotorRunning"));
}

No OnFinishedLoad, OnRegistered, or Start override on CombustionDeepMiner. _internalCombustion must exist before any atmospheric tick runs; the IsCursor guard prevents the cursor preview prefab from constructing one.

docs.title: No damage, wear, or decay on the miner itself
docs.text: No OnDamage, TakeDamage, DamageState override, or wear-state field on CombustionDeepMiner or DeepMiner. The only degradation mechanism is RPM loss (friction, stress trip, shutdown). Save/load persistence carries Throttle, CombustionLimiter, Rpm, Stress only; no implicit "miner got damaged after 10 hours of runtime" state. A miner that behaves differently today than yesterday is not doing so because of accumulated wear.

docs.title: Atmosphere / GasMixture access for chamber composition
docs.text:
The fields and methods needed to read the chamber's per-gas composition and combustion-energy potential, verified verbatim:

// Assets.Scripts.Atmospherics.Atmosphere
public MoleEnergy CombustionEnergy = MoleEnergy.Zero;   // PUBLIC FIELD, not a property

Atmosphere.CombustionEnergy is a public field, initialized to MoleEnergy.Zero. This is the value the SpeedTick gate compares against the 30 J floor (Parent.InternalAtmosphere.CombustionEnergy + _normalCombustionEnergyCache >= moleEnergy). Reading it requires Type.GetField, not Type.GetProperty.

// Assets.Scripts.Atmospherics.GasMixture
public float GetGasTypeRatio(Chemistry.GasType gasType)
{
    MoleQuantity getTotalMolesGassesAndLiquids = GetTotalMolesGassesAndLiquids;
    if (getTotalMolesGassesAndLiquids.Equals(MoleQuantity.Zero))
        return 0f;
    return gasType switch
    {
        Chemistry.GasType.Oxygen => (Oxygen.Quantity / getTotalMolesGassesAndLiquids).ToFloat(),
        Chemistry.GasType.Nitrogen => (Nitrogen.Quantity / getTotalMolesGassesAndLiquids).ToFloat(),
        Chemistry.GasType.Hydrogen => (Hydrogen.Quantity / getTotalMolesGassesAndLiquids).ToFloat(),
        Chemistry.GasType.Steam => (Steam.Quantity / getTotalMolesGassesAndLiquids).ToFloat(),
        Chemistry.GasType.CarbonDioxide => (CarbonDioxide.Quantity / getTotalMolesGassesAndLiquids).ToFloat(),
        Chemistry.GasType.NitrousOxide => (NitrousOxide.Quantity / getTotalMolesGassesAndLiquids).ToFloat(),
        Chemistry.GasType.Pollutant => (Pollutant.Quantity / getTotalMolesGassesAndLiquids).ToFloat(),
        Chemistry.GasType.Ozone => (Ozone.Quantity / getTotalMolesGassesAndLiquids).ToFloat(),
        // ...
    };
}

Per-gas ratios are read via the GasMixture.GetGasTypeRatio(Chemistry.GasType) method on the chamber's GasMixture (accessed as atm.GasMixture). Returns float in [0, 1]. Returns 0 when the chamber has no gas. There are no per-gas Ratio properties on either Atmosphere or GasMixture (no Atmosphere.RatioOxygen, no GasMixture.RatioOxygen); earlier prose that named them as if they were properties was incorrect.

// InternalCombustion (default namespace, in Assembly-CSharp)
private MoleEnergy _normalCombustionEnergyCache = MoleEnergy.Zero;

The cache is a private MoleEnergy field on the global-namespace InternalCombustion type. To resolve via reflection from a mod assembly: Type.GetType("InternalCombustion, Assembly-CSharp"), then GetField with BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.NonPublic. Harmony.AccessTools.TypeByName("InternalCombustion") also works since it scans loaded assemblies.

Set exactly once per OnAtmosphericTick, inside ManualCombust, before TryCombust runs:

public void ManualCombust()
{
    _normalCombustionEnergyCache = Parent.InternalAtmosphere.CombustionEnergy;
    Parent.InternalAtmosphere.TryCombust(...);
}

So the cache holds the pre-burn CombustionEnergy snapshot. The SpeedTick gate then sums CombustionEnergy (which TryCombust may have updated to the just-released energy) plus the cache (pre-burn potential), so the gate is effectively "did the chamber have meaningful fuel before this tick OR did this tick's burn release meaningful energy."

docs.title: InputNetwork2 is structurally present but functionally dead for this device
docs.text: DeviceInputOutput defines InputNetwork2 and the matching IsInput2Valid property. CombustionDeepMiner never reads either: OnAtmosphericTick passes only InputNetwork (not InputNetwork2) to HandleGasInput, and HandleGasInput consumes only its single parameter. The IsOperable / AssessError gates check IsInputValid (primary only), not IsInput2Valid. Consistent with the in-game observation that the miner has a single input pipe connection: the second input exists on the inheritance chain but is inert for combustion logic. Any mod that adds a second-input behaviour to this device would need to wire it explicitly.

docs.title: ConfigCartridge
docs.text: Vanilla game class for the Configuration Cartridge tablet cartridge. Builds a per-tick readout of the currently-scanned device's logic values.

docs.title: Screen plumbing
docs.text:
Source: F0117.

  • ConfigCartridge.OnScreenUpdate unconditionally writes _displayTextMesh.text = _outputText every call, then sets _scrollPanel.SetContentHeight(_displayTextMesh.preferredHeight). Any mod-side edit of the mesh text is wiped every frame and must be re-applied in a postfix.
  • ConfigCartridge._outputText is built in ReadLogicText (called from OnMainTick, slower cadence than OnScreenUpdate). Format: line 0 is ReferenceId ... $HEXID, then one line per readable LogicType emitted as {Name} ... <color=grey|green>{value}</color>. Writable uses grey, read-only uses green, ReferenceId uses #20B2AA. Separator is a literal 5-character " ... " (space, three dots, space). The {value} is the verbatim expression Math.Round(_scannedDevice.GetLogicValue(logicType), 3, MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero) appended to a StringBuilder (implicit ToString() under CultureInfo.CurrentCulture); see ../GameSystems/LogicValueFormatting.md for the cross-surface comparison.
  • ConfigCartridge.ScannedDevice is computed live from CursorManager.CursorThing as Device with an IsMasterAuthority gate. The cursor must still be pointing at the device at the instant of the click, or ScannedDevice is null and the click path bails.

docs.title: Verification history
docs.text:
• 2026-04-20: page created from the Research migration; verbatim content lifted from F0117 (ConfigCartridge-side facts). No conflicts.
  • 2026-04-26: added the verbatim format expression for {value} in the per-line emit string (Math.Round(_scannedDevice.GetLogicValue(logicType), 3, MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero), CultureInfo.CurrentCulture) and a cross-link to ../GameSystems/LogicValueFormatting.md. Re-decompiled ConfigCartridge.ReadLogicText against $(StationeersPath)\rocketstation_Data\Managed\Assembly-CSharp.dll at v0.2.6228.27061 via ilspycmd. Additive only; no contradiction with prior content.

docs.title: Open questions
docs.text: None at creation.

docs.title: Connection
docs.text: A Connection is one OpenEnd of a SmallGrid (cable, pipe, device, chute, rail). The list of OpenEnds on each SmallGrid is what ConnectedCables / ConnectedDevices / Connected iterate over to discover adjacency.

docs.title: Fully qualified type and shape
docs.text:
Assets.Scripts.Objects.Pipes.Connection (decompile line 293240). It is a plain [Serializable] POCO, NOT a MonoBehaviour and NOT a UnityEngine.Object. So it has no name property; the Connection object has no Unity identity of its own. Patches that want a human-readable identifier should use connection.Transform.gameObject.name (which is the name of the child GameObject the Connection was attached to in the prefab, e.g. OpenEnd1, Input, Output).

Verbatim shape (line 293239 onward):

[Serializable]
public class Connection
{
    public NetworkType ConnectionType = NetworkType.Pipe;
    public Transform Transform;
    public Collider Collider;
    public ConnectionRole ConnectionRole;
    [ReadOnly] public Renderer HelperRenderer;
    public SmallGrid Parent;
    public Grid3 LocalGrid;
    public Grid3 FacingGrid;
    private bool _isInitialized;
    private bool _isValid;
    public Vector3 TransformUp { get; set; }
    public bool IsValid { get { ... } }
    ...
}

Key fields for adjacency lookups:

  • ConnectionType is a NetworkType enum bitmask (Pipe / Power / Data / Chute / Rail). Used by SmallGrid.IsConnected(Connection) to gate cross-network mismatches.
  • Transform is the Unity transform of the GameObject the Connection was prefab-bound to. Transform.position is what SmallGrid.ConnectedCables() and friends pass to GridController.WorldToLocalGrid to find the adjacent SmallCell.
  • LocalGrid and FacingGrid are precomputed Grid3 coordinates: LocalGrid is the cell the Connection sits in (the one the parent SmallGrid's body occupies on that side), FacingGrid is the cell the Connection points AT (the cell beyond the body). IsConnected asserts that the other side's LocalGrid equals this side's FacingGrid.
  • Parent is the SmallGrid (Cable, Device, Pipe, etc.) that owns this Connection.
  • ConnectionRole is the directional/role tag (Input, Output, Output2, Waste, ...).

docs.title: Verification history
docs.text: • 2026-05-15: page created. Sourced from decompile line 293240 (class declaration) and the Connection field listing immediately after. Created while writing a Bug 2 diagnostic Harmony patch that needed to log per-OpenEnd identity; the patch initially used connection.name which fails to compile because Connection is a serializable POCO without a Unity name.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text: Vanilla Constructor : Stackable, IConstructionKit at Assets.Scripts.Objects.Constructor. The held-item kit form for placing a single Structure prefab. Used by every "single-item kit" (kit-cable, kit-wall-frame, kit-airlock, ...). The companion MultiConstructor covers multi-prefab kits (rail kit with 8 rail variants, etc.).

docs.title: Class layout
docs.text:
public class Constructor : Stackable, IConstructionKit
{
    [Header("Constructor")]
    public Structure BuildStructure;
    public int QuantityUsed = 1;

    public override void OnUsePrimary(Vector3 targetLocation, Quaternion targetRotation, ulong steamId, bool authoringMode)
    public virtual void Construct(Grid3 localPosition, Quaternion targetRotation, bool authoringMode, ulong steamId)
    public static Structure SpawnConstruct(CreateStructureInstance instance)
    public List<Thing> GetConstructedPrefabs()
}

BuildStructure is the prefab the kit places. QuantityUsed is how many of the stack the kit consumes per placed structure (default 1).

docs.title: OnUsePrimary -> Construct
docs.text:
public override void OnUsePrimary(Vector3 targetLocation, Quaternion targetRotation, ulong steamId, bool authoringMode)
{
    base.OnUsePrimary(targetLocation, targetRotation, steamId, authoringMode);
    Construct(targetLocation.ToGridPosition(), targetRotation, authoringMode, steamId);
}

public virtual void Construct(Grid3 localPosition, Quaternion targetRotation, bool authoringMode, ulong steamId)
{
    if (authoringMode || OnUseItem(QuantityUsed, null))
    {
        CreateStructureInstance createStructureInstance = new CreateStructureInstance(BuildStructure, localPosition, targetRotation, steamId);
        if (PaintableMaterial != null && CustomColor.Normal != null)
        {
            createStructureInstance.CustomColor = CustomColor.Index;
        }
        SpawnConstruct(createStructureInstance);
    }
}

The Quaternion parameter targetRotation flows through unchanged. authoringMode == true bypasses the OnUseItem(...) quantity-consumption check but does NOT bypass any other gate; it is purely a creative-mode item-cost waiver.

docs.title: SpawnConstruct: simulation vs client routing
docs.text:
public static Structure SpawnConstruct(CreateStructureInstance instance)
{
    if (GameManager.RunSimulation)
    {
        Structure structure = Thing.Create<Structure>(instance.Prefab, instance.WorldPosition, instance.WorldRotation, 0L);
        structure.SetStructureData(instance.LocalRotation, instance.OwnerClientId, instance.LocalGrid, instance.CustomColor);
        return structure;
    }
    if (NetworkManager.IsClient)
    {
        new ConstructionCreationMessage(instance).SendToServer();
    }
    return null;
}

Two execution branches based on simulation role:

  • GameManager.RunSimulation == true (single-player or multiplayer host): create the structure directly via Thing.Create<Structure>(prefab, worldPos, worldRot) and call SetStructureData(localRot, ...). The full Quaternion lands on the new GameObject's transform AND is mirrored into Structure.Direction (see GameSystems/PlacementOrientation.md).
  • RunSimulation == false && NetworkManager.IsClient == true (multiplayer remote client): wrap the placement intent in a ConstructionCreationMessage and send to server. The server runs the same Thing.Create + SetStructureData path inside the message's Process(hostId) handler.

Either branch preserves the Quaternion losslessly; the wire format uses full WriteQuaternion (16 bytes) per Protocols/ConstructionCreationMessage.md.

docs.title: Authoring mode
docs.text: authoringMode is enabled when the player's active hand holds an AuthoringTool (creative-mode wrench). Source: InventoryManager.cs:422 public static bool IsAuthoringMode => Instance.ActiveHand.Slot.Occupant is AuthoringTool;. The flag travels into Construct(... authoringMode ...) and only affects the OnUseItem quantity-consumption call. It does NOT bypass AllowedRotations, CanConstruct, or any other gate. A player in authoring mode still cannot place a AllowedRotations.Floor device on a wall via vanilla input; the cursor's UpdatePlacement snaps the orientation to floor before OnUsePrimary fires.

docs.title: Verification history
docs.text: • 2026-04-25: page created from a six-agent decompile pass on the placement pipeline. Source content lifted verbatim from /tmp/decompile_check/Assets/Scripts/Objects/Constructor.cs. No conflicts.

docs.text: None.

docs.title: CreateStructureInstance
docs.text: Vanilla Assets.Scripts.Networking.CreateStructureInstance. Plain managed class used as the in-process placement-intent payload for Constructor.SpawnConstruct and as the constructor source for ConstructionCreationMessage. Carries the prefab reference, target grid cell, target Quaternion, owner ID, and optional custom color index.

docs.title: Class layout
docs.text:
public class CreateStructureInstance
{
    public Grid3 LocalGrid;
    public Quaternion LocalRotation;
    public Structure Prefab;
    public GridController GridController;
    public int CustomColor = -1;
    public ulong OwnerClientId;

    public Vector3 WorldPosition => GridController.LocalToWorld(LocalGrid);
    public Quaternion WorldRotation => LocalRotation;

    public CreateStructureInstance(Structure prefabToCreate, Structure oldPrefab)
    {
        Prefab = prefabToCreate;
        LocalGrid = oldPrefab.GridController.WorldToLocal(oldPrefab.ThingTransformPosition);
        LocalRotation = oldPrefab.ThingTransformLocalRotation;
        GridController = oldPrefab.GridController;
        OwnerClientId = oldPrefab.OwnerClientId;
        CustomColor = ((oldPrefab.CustomColor != null) ? oldPrefab.CustomColor.Index : 0);
    }

    public CreateStructureInstance(Structure prefabToCreate, Grid3 localGrid, Quaternion worldRotation, ulong ownerClientId, int colorIndex = -1)
    {
        Prefab = prefabToCreate;
        GridController = GridController.World;
        LocalGrid = localGrid;
        LocalRotation = worldRotation;
        OwnerClientId = ownerClientId;
        CustomColor = colorIndex;
    }
}

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• WorldRotation is a getter aliased to LocalRotation - they are NOT separate channels in this struct. A Quaternion passed to either constructor lands in LocalRotation and surfaces unchanged on the WorldRotation getter. The ConstructionCreationMessage later writes both fields as the same value.
  • WorldPosition is computed from the grid by GridController.LocalToWorld(LocalGrid) at access time, not stored. Mutating LocalGrid after construction shifts the world position.
  • The "old prefab" constructor is used by routines that respawn / replace an existing structure (e.g. swapping a placed prefab for a variant), reading the existing object's transform rather than fresh placement input.

docs.title: CursorManager
docs.text: Vanilla game class at Assets.Scripts.CursorManager. Singleton that raycasts the player's cursor each tick and caches the Thing (or terrain feature) currently under the crosshair. Mod code that needs to answer "what is the local player looking at right now?" reads from this class rather than performing its own raycast.

docs.title: Static accessors
docs.text:
Accessor Line Returns Notes CursorThing CursorManager.cs:135 Thing Instance.FoundThing. Null when the cursor is on empty space, terrain, or a non-Thing collider; also null when the inventory UI is blocking input or the game is paused. CursorTerrain CursorManager.cs:136 Terrain feature Instance.FoundTerrain. Alternative when the cursor is on terrain. CursorHit CursorManager.cs:129 RaycastHit Most recent raycast hit (collision point, normal, collider). CursorHitMask CursorManager.cs:47 LayerMask Layer mask governing which objects are cursor-targetable.

Instance is the standard singleton backing field typical of the ManagerBase-style pattern used across the game's manager classes.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Reading the currently-hovered Thing from a BepInEx plugin's Update() or a Harmony patch:

Thing target = CursorManager.CursorThing;
if (target != null)
{
    // act on target
}

Returns directly as Thing; no cast from Interactable needed. Works identically in single-player and multiplayer: the raycast is local to each peer, so each player sees their own cursor target.

docs.title: Null-safety expectations
docs.text:
CursorThing is null-able by design. Callers MUST null-check on every read; the cursor frequently points at nothing. The returned Thing may additionally be a Unity fake-null; for maximum safety compare via !target as documented in ../Patterns/UnityFakeNull.md.

CursorManager.Instance itself may be null during very early initialization (before the scene loads); for code running after Prefab.OnPrefabsLoaded this is rarely a concern, but deep Awake-time access should guard.

docs.title: Verification history
docs.text: • 2026-04-21: page created. Findings from decompile of Assembly-CSharp.dll (CursorManager.cs line 47 for CursorHitMask, line 129 for CursorHit, line 135 for CursorThing, line 136 for CursorTerrain).

docs.title: Open questions
docs.text:
• Does CursorThing respect interactability filtering, or does it return any Thing hit by the raycast regardless of whether the player can reach / interact with it? If unreachable Things appear (for example, behind a transparent collider), callers that care must supplement with a distance / line-of-sight check.
  • Which specific UI-state flag flips CursorThing to null when "inventory UI is blocking input"? Candidates: InventoryManager.CurrentMode, a Modal.IsActive flag, or something in CursorManager.Instance directly.

docs.title: CustomScrollPanel.ScrollPanel
docs.text: In-house scroll-view component used by ConfigCartridge and other rich-text panels. Not a Unity ScrollRect; a hand-rolled MonoBehaviour with a custom lerp toward a normalized scroll target.

docs.title: Public API
docs.text:
public void SetContentHeight(float height);     // resizes the content rect
public void OnScroll(Vector2 scrollDelta);      // accumulates wheel delta into _scrollPosition
public void SetScrollPosition(float position);  // hard-snaps both target and actual

SetScrollPosition clamps position to [0..1] and writes both _scrollPosition (target) AND _scrollActualPosition (current), then calls RefreshPosition() to re-anchor the content and handle. There is no observable lerp on a SetScrollPosition call: the snap is immediate.

OnScroll writes only _scrollPosition; the visible scroll then lerps toward it inside LateUpdate at Time.deltaTime * _scrollSpeed * _sensitivity.

docs.title: Private state worth knowing
docs.text:
[SerializeField] private float _scrollPosition;        // [0..1] target the user scrolled to
private float _scrollActualPosition;                    // [0..1] current (lerps toward _scrollPosition each LateUpdate)
private float _viewportHeight;                          // cached _viewportTransform.rect.size.y
private float _contentHeight;                           // cached _contentTransform.sizeDelta.y
private float _handleHeight;
private float _effectiveSensitivity;
[SerializeField] private bool _pinContentToBottom;

_viewportHeight and _contentHeight are populated by RefreshSize(), which runs on SetContentHeight calls only. Both are zero until the first SetContentHeight call lands. Reflection-based reads must accept that early-frame reads can return zero; treat that as "no scroll needed yet."

docs.title: Visibility math
docs.text:
SetContentPosition() (line 128) sets the content rect's anchored Y to:

y = (_scrollActualPosition - (pinContentToBottom ? 1 : 0)) * (_contentHeight - _viewportHeight)

For the typical case (_pinContentToBottom == false):

  • _scrollActualPosition == 0 → top of content visible.
  • _scrollActualPosition == 1 → bottom of content visible.

The visible window covers the content's normalized Y range:

viewportFraction = _viewportHeight / _contentHeight                  // < 1 when content overflows
topFraction      = _scrollActualPosition * (1 - viewportFraction)
bottomFraction   = topFraction + viewportFraction

A point at normalized position p (where 0 = top of content, 1 = bottom) is visible iff topFraction <= p <= bottomFraction. When _contentHeight <= _viewportHeight, everything fits and any point is trivially visible.

To snap a target selFrac to the nearest visible edge (rather than dead center):

denominator = 1 - viewportFraction
if (selFrac < topFraction)    target = selFrac / denominator         // selection lands at viewport top
else if (selFrac > bottomFraction) target = (selFrac - viewportFraction) / denominator  // selection lands at viewport bottom

These derive from solving topFraction == selFrac and bottomFraction == selFrac for _scrollActualPosition.

docs.title: Reflection-based external read pattern
docs.text:
A mod that wants to ask "is content position p currently visible?" must read the three private fields by reflection:

ReflectionUtils.TryGetField<float>(panel, "_scrollActualPosition", out var actualPos);
ReflectionUtils.TryGetField<float>(panel, "_viewportHeight",       out var viewportH);
ReflectionUtils.TryGetField<float>(panel, "_contentHeight",        out var contentH);

Fail-safe pattern: if any read returns false (or returns zero on an uninitialized panel), fall back to an unconditional SetScrollPosition(selFrac) to keep the selection in view at the cost of a jolt. Visibility-aware snap is purely a UX optimization; correctness must not depend on the reflection succeeding.

docs.title: Verification history
docs.text: • 2026-04-27: page created. Verbatim findings from ilspycmd decompile of E:/Steam/steamapps/common/Stationeers/rocketstation_Data/Managed/Assembly-CSharp.dll at game version 0.2.6228.27061. Triggered by EquipmentPlus item E (viewport-aware snap on the ConfigCartridge scroll path); the mod previously called SetScrollPosition unconditionally on every wheel-tick and every postframe rebuild, which jolts the panel even when the selection is already on screen. Documents the _scrollActualPosition / _viewportHeight / _contentHeight field shape, the linear visibility math derived from SetContentPosition, and the nearest-edge snap formula.
